page: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It includes 7559 GB of local NVMe storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core stress-ng performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average range. Cryptographic and extended instruction capabilities are strong, as shown by PassMark CPU scores. For language model tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small models but exhibits poor performance when generating text with large 70B parameter models.

Given the high resource density of 1 TB of RAM paired with 32 vCPUs and over 7.5 TB of local storage, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage make it suitable for large in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. However, the average multi-core scaling and poor large-model LLM generation speeds represent clear tradeoffs for highly parallel compute or generative AI workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. It features 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core, alongside 7559 GB of local storage. Single-core performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng and single-threaded compression benchmarks placing in the top 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The server lacks GPU acceleration, resulting in poor text generation speeds for large 70B parameter language models. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and large-scale data processing, where massive RAM capacity and substantial local storage are required, but highly parallel compute scaling is not the primary constraint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring one terabyte of RAM and seven terabytes of local storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
1024.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
7559 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core scaling and poor large-model LLM text generation
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
